frameworks.name: hippocampal place coding
frameworks.one_line: single hippocampal neurons fire selectively as a function of spatial location

frameworks.name: context representation
frameworks.one_line: spatial firing patterns interpreted as encoding the behavioral/environmental context

frameworks.name: remapping
frameworks.one_line: place-field reorganization across environmental or behavioral changes

synopsis.core_question: How did the concepts of context coding and remapping in hippocampal place cells originate from cross-chamber single-unit recordings?
key_assumptions: single hippocampal neurons carry spatial information
behavior across distinct chambers can be compared at the single-neuron level
firing-pattern differences across chambers reflect a representational variable rather than noise
manipulations: behavioral task (radial-arm pellet retrieval vs. operant bar-pressing vs. maternal pup-retrieval)
recording chamber (radial-arm maze vs. operant chamber vs. large home box)
later systematic environmental/behavioral changes within the Muller collaboration
key_findings: spatial firing was observed in all three behavioral chambers
a single neuron's firing pattern in one chamber did not predict its pattern in another
interpretations: cross-chamber unpredictability of spatial firing reflects a neural representation of context
systematic exploration of these effects became the empirical basis for what is now called remapping
